facts: We use our eyes to see colors and shapes, our ears to hear sounds, our nose to smell odors, our tongue to taste flavors, and our skin to feel textures — each sense organ gives a different kind of information about an object.
An object like an apple can be identified by using multiple senses: you see its red color, feel its smooth skin, smell its fruity scent, and taste its sweetness.
What you see (sight) might not match what you feel (touch); for example, a rock that looks smooth can feel rough when you touch it.
misconceptions: A child might believe that a lemon tastes sweet because it is yellow, confusing the sense of sight (color) with the sense of taste (flavor).
